entry.title: Re: What is the replacement of IDoc in S/4HANA public cloud?
entry.content: A few Idocs are still available, mainly master data related ones. Transactional ones like ORDERS, INVOIC etc are not available anymore. You can see this if you create the Communication Arrangement for the related scenario. Mostly, IDocs are replaced by the respective SOAP services. Unfortunately, often they don't cover everything that IDocs do...

entry.title: Re: Customer has issues accessing SAP SQL Anywhere 17 EBF...
entry.content: McMartin,You are signed in with a P-user ID. Visitors with an S-user ID will benefit from more tools and enhanced functionality. If you are an SAP customer, contact a user administrator in your company to request such an ID. Your SAP Local Support Center can help you identify your administrators. If you already own an S-user ID, log out now and log in with your S-user ID.

entry.title: Re: Customer has issues accessing SAP SQL Anywhere 17 EBF...
entry.content: SAP Statement is “In the past, you would have paid for maintenance support with your SQL Anywhere purchase and you would have received a welcome letter with a unique S-User ID that would allow you to log into support.sap.com to raise incident support requests. This process has changed and maintenance support is no longer included with this product since it is now procured from our third party re-seller Digital River. Therefore, you won't get an S-User ID or support for this solution via support.sap.com anymore.” But not offering any solution to providing access to the EBF's so you are expected to buy an unsupported prroduct in buy SQL Anywhere

entry.title: Re: Error of AR29N for Real Revaluation Area separate from Orginal Depreciation Area
entry.content: For your issue, please be aware that in test mode the revaluation areas are not created, therefore the new valuation posting logic cannot be simulated and the error FAA_POST014 is triggered. Please consider create the revaluation areas by select option "No posting" indicator under the "standard revaluation" tab and run in background. Then check AR29N behavior again.

entry.title: Re: SAP business Objects following upgrade, unable to login
entry.content:
We were finally successful. 

At first we just kept disabling services and restarting SIA until we were able to consistently login.  Doing so we isolated it down to the server running "Core" services.

  • Insight to Action
  • Monitoring
  • Publishing Post Processing,
  • Publishing Service
  • Security Token Service
  • Trace Log Service
  • Translation Service

We then left this disabled create a new server and added the individual components until we found that the "Monitoring Service" was the one causing problems.  

From there we learned that under CMC-->applications-->Alerting Application, Unchecking email notifications allowed us to keep the monitoring service running; but with out email notifications. 

This made us ask why: so we dug into alerts/watches using email notifications from  BI Admin Studio.   under the: watches; there were several system generated watches which we could not get to step "Review" when editing.  This lead us to an assumption corrupt watches existed and email were somehow preventing/causing CMS to become unresponsive. 

Once we disabled the email alerts; the login issue went away completely.  Following the upgrade we deleted and re-created the services which had corrupt Watches.  (those watches which wouldn't let us get through all 4 steps + review when editing.  Now all watches can get through the "Review" step when editing.  We'll see if that helps next time; but we also presently have left off off the email alerts from Applications-->Alerting Applications within CMC. 

As to why Watches alerts would prevent login... We have no idea.  Somehow the corrupt alerts where causing the server to spin, deadlock and subsequent calls to the CMS database were hung up.  If we cleared a lock; then login would work; but there was no deadlock; just a DB waiting on the CMS server to "Finish" a transaction which it never did....

entry.title: Combine Cube Left join does not return all matching records from Right side in a 1:M relationship.
entry.content:
ENVIORNMENT: 

SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 4 Patch 2
Version: 14.3.4.4852 (WEBI)

SETUP:

Create a report which contains two data sources which relate in a 1:M fashion.

Use Combine Cube and left join to relate the data such that the data set with 1 is the primary and the data set with the M is the secondary.

Note in the results in combine cube: the record count matches the data from the left side; when one would expect the records to match that of the many side where matches were found.  In our example 1:687 so results should have 687.

Furthermore when the created cube has all records dropped onto a new report within the WEBI Document; only 1 records is displayed (we limited our data such that there is only 1 record in the primary data set; but the many dataset has 687 of records matching the joined value. yet only 1 is disabled. 

When we switch the join to be a full outer join; all records are returned from both sides as expected.

When we then filter the report to exclude records which have a "NULL" result from the primary data set side, we get the expected results. (Making the Full outer join behave like the left join should)

Basically: it looks like LEFT join is broke in a 1:M relationship... 

QUESTIONS:

  1. Are others having this issue?  
  2. Is this by design?  Should we always have the "Many" side as the Primary and the right side as the 1?
  3. If this was a M:M shouldn't it display all records from the Primary many and those that match from the secondary Many? (it's not)
  4. What can be done to mitigate it (yeah we'll upgrade to 7+ at some point but patch notes don't indicate a fix for this yet)

entry.title: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition bundle
entry.content:
GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition

Since March 2023, customers who have selected SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core ERP system to run their businesses have received additional entitlements beyond the core functionality. These added capabilities form one of two comprehensive bundles: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium.
 
In this blog, I will focus on the base edition bundle, providing a clear explanation of each entitlement and its business value. I will discuss the premium edition in a separate blog, which I will hyperlink here once created.

This blog is designed for business users and consultants with ERP experience, aiming to provide practical insights into each entitlement's purpose and when it’s most valuable. Please note, statements about adoption difficulty and required personas are based on my research and experience, and other cases may vary based on the specific business context etc.
Note 1: Hyperlinks to SAP4ME will take you directly to the relevant page if you're logged into a productive SAP4ME system and have suitable rights
Note 2: This blog speaks to the entitlements included as of 2024-10-17

Blog Outline:

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Base: Overview
  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
  4. Base Entitlement: Finance - Group Reporting
  5. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Process Automation
  6. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition
  7. Base Entitlement: BTP - CPEA Credits
  8. Base Entitlement: SAP Digital Access
  9. Base Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition
  10. Closing Remarks

 

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Base: Overview
    The Base Edition comes in two SKUs: 8014771 for global markets and 8014782 for the Greater China region (which excludes some entitlements). This blog focuses on SKU 8014771.

    How to identify if you have this SKU:
    1. Log in to SAP for Me: Reporting > Purchased Solutions
    2. Under 'Orders by Solution Area,' select S/4HANA Public - your SKU will be listed on the right. If 8014771 appears with a valid date, you have the GROW Base Edition. If 8014761 appears, you have Premium. Other SKUs likely represent SKUs that do not include this full suite of entitlements.

  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    The core entitlement includes:
    1. Services & Support: Access to support services like incident creation, onboarding advisor support, and the SAP Enterprise Support Portfolio, which offers learning content and services such as Continuous Quality Checks.
    2. Systems: Access to multiple systems (e.g., ERP production, test, and development systems) and related tenants. This also includes non-ERP systems like Cloud ALM, CBC, SAP for Me, and BTP systems.
    3. Functionality: Access to pre-configured scope items. Commonly used ones include J59 - Accounts Receivable and J60 - Accounts Payable. You can view and manage scope items through SAP Activate’s Availability and Dependencies of Scope Items accelerator.
    4. Full User Equivalent (FUE): Your contract includes a specific number of FUEs that govern how many business users can access core functionality. Check your licensed FUE quantity in SAP for Me: Reporting - License Consumption under metric CY334.
    5. Entitlement Measurement: Overuse is possible but monitored by SAP. Your account team will reach out in case of overconsumption.

  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
    What is an "entitlement"?
    An entitlement refers to the right to use a specific set of functionalities that may normally require extra payment or licenses. In the GROW Base Edition, certain features are included up to a threshold without extra software licensing costs. For example, Group Reporting is included up to 25 objects in the base edition.

    Are the "entitlements" completely free?
    The outlined entitlements mean there's no software licensing cost if you use up to the amount indicated. Usage beyond that quantity will require additional (paid) licenses, and you may need to consider some investment of business time and effort to adopt new features and functions, and in some cases you'll require consultants for the setup and implementation.

    What does "adoption" mean in this blog?
    I'm using 'adoption' to refer to the setup AND usage a software functionality. The setup or implementation is the process of adopting, and once it's setup and being actively used  - then I would then say the customer has adopted a feature/entitlement.
    Base Edition - Additional Entitlements:
    GROW with SAP Public Edition, base - additional entitlements
  4. Base Edition Entitlement: Group Reporting
    Business Case
    Group Reporting helps businesses that operate multiple subsidiaries or legal entities shorten the time needed for financial consolidation. It automates key activities and streamlines the data model, making it highly valuable for businesses with complex financial structures. For single-entity businesses, this functionality is not immediately relevant.

    Business Role Relevance
    Senior finance roles, such as Finance Managers and Group Controllers, will benefit from this functionality for managing financial consolidations.

    Entitlement
    The GROW Base Edition includes 25 Group Reporting objects. An object typically refers to a legal entity within a consolidation group (e.g., parent and subsidiary companies). Additional licenses are required for more than 25 objects. Official documentation is here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption using metric CH112 in SAP for Me under SAP4ME: Reporting -> License Consumption.

    Overusage
    It is possible to over-consume your allocated Group Reporting objects. SAP will periodically review your usage under metric CH112. You can license additional units by contacting SAP for SKU 8013710.

    Scope Item Relevance
    The main scope item for Group Reporting is 1SG. This measures whether data from group reporting has been posted into the system. You can validate its usage in Portfolio & Products - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Usage. Other Scope Items flagged as Group Reporting relevant are  287, 28B, 4R7, 2U6

    More Information
    For more detailed guidance on adopting Group Reporting, refer to this blog post. This deeper descriptions, webinar links, and crucially it outlines the key steps to adopting group reporting

    Key Adoption Notes:
    Expertise needed: You'll need a combination of BTP administrators and finance consultants for configuration and initial setup.
    Adoption Effort:  Rated 3 (on a scale from 1 to 5). You’ll need finance consultants, and technical consultants with expertise in BTP to set up this functionality.
    Not to be confused with: This entitlement does not include Group Reporting Data Collection, which must be licensed separately.

  5. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Process Automation
    Business Case
    SAP Build Process Automation enables non-developers, such as key users or business process experts, to create bots or automated workflows based on a set of rules. This is valuable when no standard ERP functionality exists to meet a specific business need, for example, automating the creation of general ledger accounts.

    Business Role Relevance
    Consultants, process automation specialists, and business key users involved in implementing and running the ERP system will find this useful.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes:
    20 Standard Users (CP278) – Can trigger or interact with a process.
    2 Advanced Users (CP279) – Can design, monitor, and administrate processes.
    1 Unattended Automation (CP283) – A bot that continuously monitors and executes a process.
    Documentation of these entitlements are here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP278, CP279, and CP283 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License

    Overusage
    You are blocked from overconsuming these licenses. Once the entitlement limit is reached, additional licenses must be purchased or allocated through CPEA credits.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no specific scope item for SAP Build Process Automation.

    More Information
    This community page has overview videos and links to all other resources. This area of the API hub is where you find SAP pre-created templates.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: You’ll need a BTP expert for the base setup. Then you'll need LoB experts e.g. finance experts to develop meaningful automations as per your needs. Setup steps are outlined here.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for platform setup by BTP experts; 2-4/5 for workflow creation depending on complexity.

  6. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition

    Business Case
    SAP Build Workzone allows organizations to create a central homepage for employees with user-specific access to applications across various SAP systems. This enhances efficiency by providing direct links to necessary business apps for roles like sales managers or purchasing clerks. The more SAP systems used throughout your organization, the more value this solution will offer your organization.

    Business Role Relevance
    This functionality benefits employees across departments who frequently use SAP applications in their daily tasks.

    Entitlement
    You receive 1 Workzone user per FUE licensed. For example, if you have licensed 100 FUEs for the core, 100 users can access SAP Build Workzone.

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP264 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License Consumption

    Overusage
    Additional licenses or CPEA credits are required if the number of users exceeds your entitlement.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no scope item related to SAP Build Workzone usage.

    More Information
    More documentation is on the SAP Build Workzone Discovery Center.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: A BTP global administrator for initial setup, followed by non-technical key users for managing pages.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for BTP experts; 3/5 for non-experts. View the setup steps in this tutorial.
    Not to be confused with: SAP Build Workzone is distinct from SAP Start, which a more lightweight solution without ability to consider other SAP system authorizations. SAP Build Workzone Advanced Edition is a different product without a migration path from the standard edition.

  7. Base Edition Entitlement: CPEA Credits

    Business Case
    CPEA Credits (Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement) allow businesses to consume various BTP services within predefined limits without additional fees. These credits can be applied to services like Integration Suite, SAP Build Apps, and Cloud Identity Services. You can view the list of included services here in the Discovery Center. There's more nuance to the CPEA credit commercial model, which you can read about here.

    Business Role Relevance
    Technical teams managing BTP services will oversee consumption of this entitlement. The business areas that consume this depend on which services you're utilizing from BTP - many are in the technical / administration area.

    Entitlement
    Customers receive 1% of their net Annual Contract Value in CPEA credits, with a minimum value of €2,000 and a maximum value of €16,000.

    License Consumption
    You can view your CPEA credit balance in SAP4ME - Finance and Legal - Enterprise Agreements

    Scope Item Relevance
    CPEA credit usage is not tied to any scope item, as it applies across various BTP services.

    More Information
    Each service that can be paid for by CPEA credits are listed here in the Discovery Center - and this same resource provides additional information on each service.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP experts and business area owners are required to manage and utilize CPEA credits effectively.
    Adoption Effort: Varies depending on the BTP service being used.
    Not to be confused with: CPEA credits differ from BTPEA and EA credits, which operate similarly but cover different services.

  8. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Digital Access

    Business Case
    SAP Digital Access governs how non-human interactions (such as external systems creating documents) are licensed. It is relevant when integrating third-party systems like Salesforce to trigger document creation within SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

    Business Role Relevance
    IT and integration specialists responsible for system integrations with non-SAP platforms will need to manage this entitlement.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes 100,000 digital access documents per year.

    License Consumption
    Track your consumption by launching the License Compliance Digital Access app in the productive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This guidance document shows how to do this and provides other useful context on measuring your digital access usage for SAP S/4HANA

    Overusage
    It is possible to exceed your annual document allowance. SAP periodically reviews your consumption and the account team will reach out in the case of overconsumption.

    Scope Item Relevance
    This entitlement applies to external system interactions and is not tied to specific scope items.

    More Information
    Refer to the Digital Access documentation for a more detailed explanation.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: No specific competency is needed. Entitlement consumption should be reviewed by the party responsible for your SAP system billing & commercials.
    Adoption Effort: 1. There's nothing to setup per se for Digital Access - you only be aware of it and monitor your consumption.
    Other Notes: SAP Digital Access is only necessary for non-SAP systems. If SAP systems trigger document creation, digital access is not consumed.

  9. Base Edition Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition

    Business Case
    Ariba Buying Base Edition allows employees to make internal purchase requisitions. It's scope is similar to the core ERP requisitioning scope of 18J, but Ariba Buying Base has an enhanced UX and has the capability to integrate with many advanced features, such as directly connecting to supplier catalogs and the Ariba Business network.

    Business Role Relevance
    All company employees can benefit from an improved user experience for purchase requisitions, particularly in organizations using self-service requisitioning.

    Entitlement
    There is no access limit for the base functionality, but additional licensing may be required for extended capabilities, such as linking to external supplier catalogs.

    License Consumption
    There are no limits to usage of this entitlement. It operates more like a standard scope item, but many of the functionalities that bring Ariba Buying Base to life will require additional licenses.

    Scope Item Relevance
    Scope item 4AI measures whether you've setup the communications arrangement required to use Ariba Buying base edition.

    More Information
    View an overview of Ariba Buying in this video demo - but note this video includes the utilization of catalogs that may require additional licensing.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP administrators and procurement experts are required for setup and catalog integration.
    Adoption Effort: Unclear
    Other Notes: A lot of the functionality’s value comes from adding catalogs and integrating with the broader Ariba Network, which may require additional licensing.

  10. Closing
    The intent of this blog was to give you a base understanding of the each entitlement in the base edition - what it is, and when it's most valuable.
    Any feedback regarding on the content including other use cases, other resources to link etc. are welcome and I'll periodically update this blog with improved information.

entry.title: Re: xsls file from AL11 convert to internal table in SAP...
entry.content:
0.07 corresponds to 7.000000009E-2 because Excel stores the numbers as binary floating-point numbers in 8 bytes, hence it's unable to store exactly decimal numbers. Differently said, storing a decimal (base 10) number in a binary (base 2) number goes with imprecision for the decimals. There is also the limit of the number of significant digits.

You may transfer the number into a P variable with few decimals, that will round the number correctly.

entry.title: Re: How to remove 'Application deletes' SOST (RSCBS_REORG)
entry.content: 3499226 - RSBCS_REORG shows entries with "Application Deletes" and "APPL_ID: PPFB" which cannot be deleted by the report When there are entries such as "Application Deletes" in output of RSBCS_REORG, the reports from the SAPoffice side will never delete the data. The Application that generated the documents must first delete the document links, then RSBCS_REORG will completely delete the documents. There is an application which has PPF integrated. This application executes PPF actions, which (sometimes) will result in the creation of a send requests (e-mail). As a security measure this send request is marked via the term ‘PPFB’ that it was created through a PPF action. This will prevent the accidently deletion of such send requests via the report RSBCS_REORG. So to delete such entries the corresponding actions which were responsible for creation of such mails need to be deleted. This will trigger the necessary routines and then the deletion with the next run of RSBCS_REORG is possible. The PPF framework does not delete any action automatically at any point because it cannot decide whether the deletion of the action and its connected data (like an e-mail) is required. Therefore the application has routines to delete old/unneeded actions or mechanisms to archive them. You should decide the set of the time frame for the deletion, also please use cation when selecting for deletion. (Legal point of view, audit etc.) Resolution 1. First run the report RSPPFPROCESS for deletion of data in Date Range of your choice (set the 'Allow deletion of Actions' at the Processing Settings, so it will delete the actions.) More details in the report found in 896858 - FAQ: SPPFP - Report RSPPFPROCESS about the mentioned report. 2. Next Execute the report RSBCS_REORG for the same period, the "Application Deletes" entries with "APPL_ID: PPFB" should then be deleted.

entry.title: How to use FILTER condition in HTTP Receiver Adapter for SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Techies, 

 

I would like to bring up here one interesting Topic in this blog, Which I have faced few issues while using the HTTP Receiver Adapter in SAP CPI.

I have faced issues with the OData Adapter that GET was not successful due to some Conversion of date with Exception. So decided to use HTTP Adapter instead of OData.

By using OData adapter its pretty simple that we can filter condition while configuring the fields in OData fields selection level and based on the selected fields and filters query will be generated automatically.

But Incase of HTTP Adapter we need to manually form the Query in Param1=Value1&Param2=Value2 like this. This format will work if I want to use only with field level Query.

I want to use Filter in the Query tab I have used below format
$filter=Material='1234' 

$filter=Materialeq'1234'  but no luck 😞

Finally after so many trail and error methods below format is working and its able to fetch only particular Materials.

$filter=Material%20eq%20'1234'

The formation of the query should be separated with %20 before and after eq

So, by using the above format we can the Filter condition in HTTP Receiver condition and also SKIP and TOP also will be accepted by HTTP Adapter Query level.

$filter=Material%20eq%20'1234'&$top=10&$skip=5

 

Thanks & Regards, 

SaiBandaru

SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration

SAP S/4HANA SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration

entry.title: Authorization Management Service in SAP Cloud Identity Service
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) is transitioning from using XSUAA for authorization management to a more integrated solution with Authorization Management Service (AMS). AMS is a pivotal component within the SAP Cloud Identity Service, designed to streamline and centralize the management of end-user authorizations for applications. This service ensures that individuals have appropriate access to the resources and functionalities they need to perform their roles effectively while mitigating security risks.

Firstly, many applications define authorizations internally to meet their specific domain needs. These internal mechanisms ensure tailored security but can lead to a decentralized and fragmented access management system. To address this, SAP Cloud Identity Services offers a solution by centralizing user assignments to roles and groups, significantly simplifying the management of user access.

The Authorization Management Service builds on this by providing a unified platform for managing authorizations across applications within SAP BTP. AMS integrates seamlessly with SAP Cloud Identity Services, enabling administrators to configure and assign policies through a centralized console. This integration enhances the efficiency and consistency of policy application across different services and users.

At the core of AMS is policy assignment. Within SAP Cloud Identity, each policy aligns with a group in the Identity Directory, and these policies can be linked to users by assigning them to the appropriate group. SAP offers flexibility in this process, allowing customers to use both predefined SAP policies and custom policies, which can be managed either through the Cloud Identity console or programmatically via the SCIM API. This ensures a scalable and adaptable authorization management system tailored to organizational needs. 

Authorization Management Service Architecture

By centralizing authorization management, AMS offers several key benefits:

  • Improved Efficiency: Streamlined processes for assigning and managing authorizations reduce administrative overhead.
  • Enhanced Security: Centralized control over access helps prevent unauthorized access and data breaches.
  • Simplified Compliance: AMS can assist organizations in meeting regulatory requirements related to data privacy and security.
  • Flexibility: Customers can choose from pre-defined SAP policies or create custom policies to suit their specific needs.

What is a policy? 

A policy in the context of Authorization Management Service (AMS) is a structured set of rules that governs whether a user or group of users is allowed to perform specific actions on a resource, depending on certain conditions. These conditions can be based on user attributes or data from the business application.

In AMS, policies externalize authorization decisions to the AMS runtime engine, which evaluates relevant policies for a particular authorization request. Each policy defines the type of access (such as "Read") to a specific resource (like a business object) and is evaluated at runtime based on conditions set by administrators, who can adjust restrictions as needed. This allows for flexible and dynamic control over resource access within business applications.

Here are some example policies in Data Control Language (DCL) with actions on special resources where additionally attribute values restrict the user authorization. 

In the following sections, we will delve deeper into the functionalities of AMS, including its integration with SAP Cloud Identity Services, policy assignment mechanisms, and how it contributes to overall application security. 

Authorization Management for Cloud Identity Service

The Authorization Management Service (AMS) is now integrated into the user management console of SAP Cloud Identity, enabling customers to implement fine-grained access control for delegated administration. This feature allows organizations to restrict the authorizations of user management administrators to a specific subset of users or attributes within the user base. As a result, customers can delegate administrative tasks to local or specialized administrators without granting full access to all user data.

In some cases, the default administrator authorizations provided in the SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant may be too broad, offering unrestricted data access. For scenarios requiring more nuanced control, such as attribute-based access control (ABAC), customers can define custom authorization models with detailed instance-level restrictions. Administrators create authorization policies using user attributes and assign these policies to other administrators, enabling one administrator to manage only a specific subset of users or user attributes. Similarly, administrators can define policies using application organization IDs to restrict access to certain applications.

By leveraging policies, customers can configure precise access control for SAP Cloud Identity Services administrators. To activate this feature, it must first be enabled via the SAP Cloud Identity admin console. Once enabled, the "Authorization Policies" tab will appear within approximately 60 seconds in the administration console.

Initially, only the base policies are visible. You can create new authorization policies on the base policies and assign them to administrators. The custom policies will also appear in the list along with the base policies. 

1. Configure User Authorizations

Let me show you an example that how to define a policy and assign the policy to an administrator. Sue Smith is an administrator of SAP Cloud Identity Services, and she is responsible for the branch office in the USA. As such she needs to have access only to the users in the USA. When you create a new policy, you can restrict the users on the basis of the following attributes: user.name, country, costCenter, division, department, and organization. In this example, you can choose country to restrict management of the user only in the USA.

Note: The addresses must be marked as primary via the Identity Directory SCIM REST API. Users who don't have a primary address are excluded even if the user.addresses.country attribute matches the address of the user.

 Once the rule definition is completed, you can assign this policy to Sue Smith. 

Last but not least, you also need to remove the Read Users and Manage Users authorizations that Sue has as an administrator. 

2. Configure Application Authorizations

You can also configure new policies to allow the administrator manage a subset of applications in SAP Cloud Identity Services. When you create a new policy, you can restrict the users on the basis of the attribute application.organization. By default, all applications are in the global organization. You can edit the Organization ID of the application and define a custom policy. 

Authorization Management for Custom Business Applications

The Authorization Management Service (AMS) as part of SAP Cloud Identity Services (SCI) provides libraries and tools that enable developers of cloud business applications to define, enforce, and manage instance-based authorization checks. When integrated with the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model, AMS policies can include CAP roles along with additional filter criteria for instance-based authorizations that can be defined in the CAP model. These authorizations are then transformed into AMS policies, which can later be refined by authorization administrators in the SCI administration console and assigned to business users.

For developers, managing authorizations is a crucial but often complex task. Proper authorization ensures that end users have controlled access to critical data and functionality, protecting the company from potential harm caused by mistakes or malicious actions. However, finding the right balance of access control can be challenging and time-consuming.

The new Authorization Management Service simplifies this process for developers working on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). AMS provides a centralized system for managing end-user authorizations for business applications built on SAP BTP. It seamlessly integrates with SAP's development models, particularly the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model. When defining an authorization policy, developers can specify the conditions under which access is granted and whether these conditions can be adjusted later. Once the application is deployed on SAP BTP, administrators can further customize these authorization policies with specific conditions based on the business context, allowing for centralized, fine-tuned access control through a user-friendly interface.

Augment SAP BTP applications with AMS

The following steps are necessary to develop a CAP application with AMS:

  1. Add the latest version of @SAP/ams and @SAP/ams-dev dependencies to package.json.
  2. Annotate your services and entities with standard @requires and @restrict CAP annotations for role-based access control (RBAC).
  3. (Optional) Add @AMS.publicFields annotations to be able to write attribute-based access control (ABAC) policies in Data Control Language (DCL), the AMS-specific language used to express authorization policies.
  4. (Optional) Use @AMS.alias annotations to decouple your entity design decisions from abstract resources used in your policies. This leaves you free to change your entities later without affecting existing policies.
  5. Run your application with cds watch. The ams development plugin (@SAP/ams-dev) will compile your cds model along with the AMS annotations to Data Control Language (DCL). Alternatively, generate the DCL files manually via cds build --for ams (or during a full cds build).

    Now, you can see that in gen/srv/ams/dcl/cap/basePolicies.dcl the base policy admin and Reader is generated. A user with the policy cap.admin can access the endpoints of the AdminService, a user with the policy cap.Reader can see all the books from the CatalogService. 

    You can observe that a folder with name ams is generated in your root folder. This is where we can define our schema extensions and custom policies. By default, the folder is empty. In the following example, we define a custom policy JuniorReader in the cap folder. 

  6. The AMS runtime plugin (@SAP/ams) enforces your authorization policies to secure the application. Once the application is deployed to Cloud Foundry, you can manage the AMS policies in the Cloud Identity Service admin console. Find the deployed application under Applications tab and assign policies to users under Authorization Policies. 

Summary

For newly built applications, using Authorization Management Service (AMS) is generally recommended. The primary requirement for using AMS is that users must be replicated to the Identity Directory Service, which serves as the central hub for managing users in SAP BTP applications. This aligns with SAP's broader strategy to simplify and streamline user management across its platform in the future. 

More information

  • Configuring Authorization Policies
  • Assign Authorization Policies
  • CAP Developer Guide
  • Github Samples

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 17th, 2024
entry.content:
ITEMS

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model September 2024 Release 

  • Details in Capire https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/sep24  

SAP Fiori Innovation Day 

  • SAP Fiori Innovation Day https://events.sap.com/eur/sap-fiori-innovation-day-heidelberg-2024/en/home 

SAP Cloud Integration News 

  • Message Status Overview in Cloud Integration https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/monitor-message-status-overview  
  • SMB Adapter in Cloud Integration https://api.sap.com/package/SMBAdapterforSAPIntegrationSuite/overview 
  • Message Transform utilities in Cloud Integration https://api.sap.com/package/com.sap.integration.cloud.utilities.MessageTransformations/overview 

Flexso Tech Day 2024 

  • Event website https://www.flexso.com/en/events/tech-day-2024  

Devtoberfest Week 4 

  • Devtoberfest sessio¡n calendar https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true 
  • Devtoberfest contest https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-contest/ba-p/13781593 

================================================================================ 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 
0:07 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model September 2024 Release 
2:28 SAP Fiori Innovation Day 
3:10 SAP Cloud Integration News 
4:26 Flexso Tech Day 2024 
5:22 Devtoberfest Week 4 

TRANSCRIPT

[Ajay] Hello, developers, September 2024 release of CAP is now available. The first update is about analytics for Capire Matomo is used to collect anonymized usage data for Capire, and users will have the option to disable the tracking. Coming to the Node.js updates, you can now import type definitions generated by the CDS typer on the top level code, even in the test code. Now we have richer JavaScript and TypeScript snippets in the compiler, which can now carry type information, allowing you to get a better insight into the APIs used there. Instance-based restrictions for bound actions and functions are now also enforced. SAP Cloud Application Event Hub is the new default offering for messaging in SAP Business Technology Platform, Integrating with SAP Cloud Application Event Hub is provided via a CDS plugin @cap-js/event-broker. Moving on to Java updates, we have enhancement to the code generator, where we now have stricter setters in the accessor interfaces. Accessor interfaces now offer the new factory method to access the data in the given map. Monitoring health status of MTX Sidecar is enabled with a new health indicator model provider. Parameter aliases in OData V4 for the entity key values and function parameter values are now also supported. You can easily add handler stubs using add handler CLI command in Java. A new serious property, ui5.version allows you to configure UI5 versions used by the Fiori preview. Finally, in the tools update, we have got beta release to visualize deployment descriptor mta.yaml file. This helps you to get a graphical overview of your microservices architecture and the interplay between the application and the services. This also works as a visual linter. There are two new experimental CDS lint checks, sql-null-comparison and no-java-keywords. For more details, please check the links provided in the description.

[Nico] Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. The SAP Fiori Innovation Day will happen on November 19th in Heidelberg, Germany in the SAP App Haus, interestingly the same location we were at for SAP TechEd Virtual a couple of weeks ago. The SAP Fiori Innovation Day is open for everyone, and it will be a packed day with content about the SAP Fiori design system, SAP Fiori elements, AI of course, and it will also contain partner sessions. There will also be a hands-on session about the SAP Fiori elements flexible programming model. Check out the link in the description for more information and to register. See you. Bye.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. I have some exciting news to share with you today. The message status overview that we are so used to in SAP PI/PO is now available in SAP Integration Suite. The message status overview page provides a customizable overview of the messages process and their statuses in a selected time frame, as well as quick navigation to and from the message monitoring in your tenant. Also, there is a new adapter available to us in Cloud Integration. This is the SMB adapter, which will allow you to connect to a server using the SMB protocol, meaning that we can interact with an SMB server and read and write files. This is especially relevant for those on-premises integration scenarios. And finally, there is now an alternative solution in Cloud Integration to the MessageTransformBean adapter module that we often use in SAP PI/PO to aid with the transformation of the message during the runtime. It is in the form of Groovy scripts, which support most of the features that existed on the MTB module. Make sure to check it out. Ciao.

[DJ] If you're in or can get to the Brussels / Mechelen area of Belgium in early November, then there's a Tech Day happening on the latest SAP tools and technologies. Flexo Tech Day 2024 is happening on Thursday, the 7th of November. And following the welcome and keynote, There are sessions across three separate tracks, analytics and integration, extensions, and there's also a trending track. Now, the full agenda is online. The day is open to everyone, but spaces are limited. So head on over to the website to register. And at the end of the day, there's gonna be a beer tasting, mostly because the day is being held in a brewery. What's not to like? I'll be there. See you there too.

[Nico] Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. We're just wrapping up week three of Devtoberfest, which means there's just one week left for Devtoberfest 2024. So next week we'll have five more days of packed content, all about SAP development. So on Monday, it will be ABAP and CAP again. On Tuesday, Tooling Tuesday, we'll talk about the SAP Build family. Wednesday is Integration Day. On Thursday we talk about MAD, which is machine learning, AI and data. And Friday will be Frontend Friday as well as Fun Friday. And for Fun Friday next week there will be Spooktoberfest, so expect a spooky day. Overall, next week I counted 24 sessions scheduled, that's a lot of content and it's great content too. We hope to see many more familiar faces as well as new faces. Welcome if you're new to this channel. Now's your chance to earn points in the Devtoberfest gameboard and to progress towards Nerdvana, which would qualify you for the Devtoberfest grand prize. So hope to see you around and see you next time.

entry.title: How do I reset RSMDATASTATE after a load to a Datamart is decommissioned?
entry.content:
Here is the context and background for my question:

The System is BW 7.52 on Hana. I have a base cube (ZBase_CB1) that was used as the source to feed a secondary cube (ZCO1234). The feed was performed via a delta DTP, but now that load has been decommissioned. I cannot delete ZCO1234 because the static data might be needed. But the problem is such that I cannot compress ZBase_CB1 because the system tells me all datamarts have not been updated.

Request 7,563,690 not datamarted to all data targets. Please do this first.

Message no. RSM1548

What is the proper method to remediate this issue permanently?  In my sandbox and development instance, I can update the DMALL field of RSMDATASTATE which seems to fix the issue, but I cannot do the same in a closed system.

entry.content:
Hi, I'm getting an unusual error when doing the Goods Issue (261) Production order. It's an independent Production order without sales order or forecast, it was manually created production order, but the error doesn't make sense. It's asking for Partner role SH missing. Could you please help?

  1. Transaction MIGO
  2. Goods issue 261 
  3. Enter Production order
  4. Set flag "ITEM OK"
  5. Click on "Check" button (Document is ok)
  6. Click on "Post" button
  7. The system issues the below error:

Error during copy of partner: Partner role SH. Message no. VL864

entry.title: Re: Inquiry About the Origin of Contact Upload
entry.content: You can check from your side looking at the field "Contact source" on contact profile according to the document Overview of the Contact creation and Contact Source field

entry.title: Re: ERROR=CANNOT USE DUPLICATE TRIGGER NAME
entry.content: It's possible that a job could fail and restart, causing the dump to be imported twice. However, if the schema was truly empty before the import, this shouldn't cause a duplicate trigger error. You can check the job logs to see if this is happening.

entry.title: Re: Modify mail text in work item
entry.content:
I would check the workflow log details (as well as ST22 and SM58). In the workflow log, you should be able to see if there were any exceptions which caused the delay in sending email. The same goes for ST22 and SM58.

I don't know if you are using the SAP delivered workflow template with this task, or if you have created a custom template.  I also do not know what your customizing of release strategies looks like.    Information like this could be helpful in solving your problem.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud : Create New Company Code.
entry.content:
Dear All

We have activated the Best Practices with Demo Data and Organizational Structure and confirmed all activities within SAP Public Cloud CBC whereas all settings and data have been deployed successfully to starter evaluation system.

Now to check the cross-company code scenarios, we have created a new Company Code, Plant, Sale Organization, Sale Office, Division, Distribution Channel, Storage Locations and Purchase Organization within Organizational Structure Node of CBC system and completed/confirmed the last step. Deployment Status is also showing completed successfully. 

However, within Starter Customizing System , we are not able to view the newly created company codes, plants , sale organizations in any App, except the company code , sale orgs which was available by default with activation of best practices.

Kinly guide, how to move the new created company codes to Starter Customizing system as last step of defining scope in CBC is also having status completed but there is no impact on starter system.

entry.title: Re: sending email to multiple person in workflow
entry.content: This is standard WF functionality - that any one of the agents of a specific task (which is not an email) can access the workitem, and it is removed from the inboxes of others. If this is not how your process should work, then you could create multiple consecutive approval tasks, with each one being assigned to a different set of users.

entry.title: App Extensibility: Manage Product Master via CDS Extension leads to Gateway Error
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I need to add the filed kzkup from the MARC Table to Manage Product Master App. The Field is available in the CDS View I_ProductPlantBasic with the name  IsCoProduct. Its not available in the views C_Productplant and I_ProductPlantWD. Therefore I want to bring it to these views via CDS Extension. Doing this leads to an internal server. 

C_Productplant is an extensible CDS view which is explicitly mentioned in the extensbilty help for the app:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/bc6b9325fedd4344a84412b2195064fa/ecb59556027ed75fe10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

Here is my coding for the extension of I_ProductPlantWD

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZIPRDPLANTWD_EXT'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for I_ProductPlantWD'
extend view I_ProductPlantWD with ZI_ProductPlantWD_EXT
{
@ObjectModel.readOnly: 'EXTERNAL_CALCULATION'
    Plant.IsCoProduct
}

 

Here is my coding for the extension of C_Productplant

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZCPRDPLANT_EXT'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension for C_Productplant_EXT'
extend view C_Productplant with ZC_Productplant_EXT
{
    @ObjectModel.readOnly: true
     Plant.IsCoProduct
}


This leads to the following error in the service sap/opu/odata/sap/MD_C_PRODUCT_MAINTAIN_SRV/ :

<Error_Resolution>
<SAP_Transaction>For backend administrators: use ADT feed reader "SAP Gateway Error Log" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details</SAP_Transaction>
<SAP_Note>See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)</SAP_Note>
</Error_Resolution>
</innererror>
</error>

/IWFND/ERROR_LOG shows:

<ERROR_CONTEXT>

entry.title: Re: How to manually set message tatus in CPI MessageProce...
entry.content:
Hi Andrejs,

No cannot override the standard message status , that is set on the type of end event you use in flow .

However you can use property SAP_MessageProcessingLogCustom and set any custom value to this this won't change main status but will add additional status in log.

Thanks,

Manoj
